original text:
We will explore the global high-end market for Japanese food.
The Japanese food industry is reputable even overseas with its high quality, safety and security. The industry has been nurtured throughout Japan and there are lots of unique and hidden products with a long history in local cities.
Our aim is to educate the world about the culinary delights of Japan, even to those foreign consumers who have never tasted Japanese food. It is the mission of Zelico to contribute delivering the delight of tasting Japanese food to people around the world.
We will convey the excellence of Japanese foods to nearly 200 countries.
Each country with its unique history, custom and natural resources has an individual food culture.
We will convey the excellence of Japanese food to people around the world while tailoring our products to each individual world market.
We are dedicated to increase the world' s recognition for the Japanese food culture while delivering an appreciation for Japanese food to people around the world.
This is what our primary vision is all about.
We aim to achieve sales to the value of \10 billion in the medium-to long-term through exporting Japanese food to nearly 200 countries.
throughout the world focusing on Asia, European & American market.
1 Export to Asian, European & American and other countries.
2 Third ‐ country trade of the products produced under Japanese quality control.
3 Import of the products from all of the world.
Corporate Name Suzuki Eikodo Inc.
From the foundation in 1877, production of candies and jellies including "Zelico" .
Planning, purchasing, and selling of confectioneries to amusement parks and facilities.
Planning, importing, and selling of stuffed toys, key chains, and gifts to amusement parks and facilities.
1877 Founded as a confectioner in Tawara town, Oogaki city by Mr. Kamesaburo Suzuki.
The completion of the company building.
Started the production of jelly.
1933 Incorporated. Mr. Denshichi Suzuki assumed the position of first president. The company capitalized at 50 thousand yen.
1935 The new building was extended. Started the production of Zelico Candy.
1939 Started to export strawberry jelly to Hawaii.
1948 The first president, Mr. Denshichi Suzuki assumed the position of Oogaki Chamber of Commerce and Industry' s vice president.
1951 Built Terauchi Town Storehouse at Terauchi town, Oogaki city.
1955 Launched the nationwide Zelico promotional tour by advertising vans.
1960 Increased its capital to 3 million yen. The first president, Mr. Denshichi Suzuki won the Honorary President Award at the 16th National Confectionery Exposition for the whole of his great achievements in the industrial development of confectionery.
1961 Mr. Denshichi Suzuki won the Honorary President Award from National Small and Medium-sized Enterprises Association.
1971 Started the import of stuffed toy, metal/wooden toys and so forth.
1976 Mr. Seijiro Suzuki assumed the position of third president.
1989 Achieved sales of 1 billion yen.
2004 Built the second factory at Asakusa town, Oogaki city.
 Den Suzuki assumed the position of fourth president. Eagle Seika Inc. became a wholly owned subsidiary company.
Built the new Yoro Distribution Center.
Increased the capital to 30 million yen.
Founded Eikodo Shanghai with 1 million CNY in capital.
Built the new jelly production factory at Iga city, Mie prefecture.
Founded EIKODO VIETNAM CO.LTD with 500,000 USD in capital.
Chambord Inc. became wholly owned subsidiary company.
Increased the capital to 50 million yen.
Tokyo Dream Inc.

original text:
Hop on a helicopter and enjoy a spectacular aerial view of the Coast Mountains. Come see the famous backcountry of Whistler BC while soaring over mountains, lakes, volcanoes, and more! And, for those who feel exceptionally brave, take an adventure with a Glacier Landing and explore a side of Whistler so few will ever see.
Book Blackcomb Helicopters Calendar Click here for quick booking!
Chevron down What will I see on a helicopter trip?
On the introductory helicopter trip, you'll explore the incredible surroundings of the Whistler area.
On the 24-minute trip, you'll enjoy a bird's eye view of the Whistler area while flying by the incredibly beautiful Cheakamus Glacier and Black Tusk. Also included is a 15-minute glacial landing.
Chevron down Can I bring my kids along?
Yes! If your kids are under the age of 2, they fly for free on a parent's lap.
Chevron down What happens if I book a trip on a stormy day?
All helicopter trips are subject to weather conditions, availability or aircraft, and civil emergencies. If on the day of your trip the weather is determined to be unsafe to fly, your trip will be cancelled and no charges will apply.
Chevron down Do you provide shuttle service?
 Free shuttle is available from Whistler Village to the Whistler Heliport for up to 14 passengers.
Chevron down Is there an additional charge for glacier landings?
Absolutely not! Glacier landings are 100% complementary and dependent on weather conditions.
Chevron down How many people can go into the helicoptor per trip?
Helicopter seats max. 5 or 6 persons.

original text:
Fonts.com · This site has endless directories of great fonts. Instant access and unlimited downloads to 2,500,000 fonts. You know we are really cheap. So, why are we recommending a fee-based site? Quantity, quality, and the convenience of easily finding exactly what you need quickly.
Pizza Dude · You'll find a great variety here.
Font Garden · Lots of unique fonts and dingbats.
Blambot! · Free comic fonts.
Cheap Skate Fonts · If your looking for something out of the ordinary, you've found it.
Tepid Monkey Fonts · Freeware fonts for a freeware world.
DA3 · View and download over 100 free fonts.
Type Euphoria · Fonts and more fonts.
Dog Hause · When you thought you had all the fonts you needed, get some pet fonts.
Font-a-licious Fonts · Fonts and dingbats in both PC and Mac downloadable format.
Phantomphonts · Something new for your PC or Mac.
 · Lots of thought went into the title of this one.
Media Builder · Over 500 fonts, but that's not all. Look for free graphics and more.
Caffeen Fonts · Cool free fonts.
Graphic Park · Great unique fonts from this graphic design company.
Fontmaster · The master of the fonts.
Yet Another Font Site · Ok content, but a great name.
Plucer Vision · Lots o' fonts.
StimulEye Fonts · This site really is a stimulus for your eyes.
Core · Some are free to download, others you'll need to buy.
The Font Pool · With over 1000 categorized truetype fonts for you to download, with previews and charactermaps.
com · Be sure to check out "Type 101" where they have assembled some of the best typographic resources for those who are just getting started in the world of typography. Over 8,000 quality (not free) fonts. List of Font Designers, Foundries, Tools, Resources, Associations, Organizations and even Events.

original text:
Answers to Everyday Questions : Is there going to be a Vampire Diaries season 3 and do you know any spoilers for the next episodes?
I know it is only half way through season 2 but is it going to continue to 3 and so on?
Also I am up to the episode the descent but it doesn't come on for another 2 weeks and I have been waiting so long already! Do you know any spoilers for season 2?
Is there much going on in Vienna between Christmas and New Year? Are shops open etc?
Im going on medication for depression within the next month, what brands have a side effect of weightloss?
Are there going to be thanksgiving sales in places like American Eagle or Abercrombie?
When is Justin Bieber going to Massachusetts and is there anyway to win tickets?
There is no such news that CW has renewed the show for season 2 in my opinion.
im not sure if theres going to be a 3rd seaso but i hope so cuz the show is GREAT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

original text:
I am suddenly having trouble sending photos (even small ones - 400Kb for example) by e-mail. My e-mail provider (Telewest Blueyonder in the UK)is splitting the messages up into tiny bites and as many as 32 are sent - and received as gibberish. Help please!
I only ask because I'm on Blue Yonder too, and yesterday their system was up and down like a yo-yo!
It still hadn't sorted itself out when I left at 8am this morning, so if this was only a problem for you yesterday, it may be them and not you.
Have you checked the engineering section on the website? Asked them by email?
by mmogano · 13 years ago In reply to Was this only yesterday?
Hi and thank u. No, it has been happening for some weeks but I think it is more related to Outlook Express than Blueyonder - but I may be wrong. Would be interested to hear what they tell you. Thanks.
